failure_modes.text: A satisfying blame story can arrive before the real causal structure is understood.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When a Fast Blame Story Replaces Causal Inquiry
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate symptom, proximate trigger, and root cause explicitly before accepting any blame story; satisfying explanations arrive fastest but are often wrong.

failure_modes.text: The model fails when symptom, proximate trigger, and root cause get collapsed into one emotionally available explanation.
failure_modes.source_quote: distinguish symptoms, proximate causes, and root causes
failure_modes.mitigation: Use structured root-cause techniques (fishbone, five whys) to resist collapsing multiple causal layers into one emotionally available explanation.

failure_modes.text: Attribution resistance becomes avoidance when the team keeps suspending judgment after the causal evidence is already sufficient.
failure_modes.source_quote: resist premature attribution, not responsible action forever
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a decision threshold: once sufficient causal evidence exists, commit to an attribution rather than letting resistance become avoidance.

premortem_questions.text: Are we explaining the outcome with the most available story or with the best-supported cause chain?
premortem_questions.source_quote: the first story is not always the right cause

premortem_questions.text: What is symptom, what is proximate trigger, and what is root cause here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: distinguish symptoms, proximate causes, and root causes

premortem_questions.text: What incentives or structures might be generating the behavior we are tempted to personalize?
premortem_questions.source_quote: look at incentives and structures, not just visible actors

heuristics.text: Use root-cause discipline before assigning responsibility so the team does not confuse explanation with blame.
heuristics.source_quote: causal inquiry should precede confident attribution

heuristics.text: Run Five Whys or similar cause-tracing methods to push past the first narratively satisfying answer.
Check whether incentives and system conditions are producing behavior that looks purely personal at first glance.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on anti-blame narrative resistance, proximate-versus-root diagnosis, and structural cause tracing.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor causality sprawl because the raw source is stronger on resisting fast attribution and tracing hidden structure.
open_questions: The first heuristic compresses repeated cautions that accountability should follow better diagnosis, not replace it.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on root-cause-analysis, systems-thinking, critical-thinking, and first-principles-thinking as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on confirmation-bias and blame-story narrative closure, but only current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has an explicit tension block around incentives and confirmation-bias plus a non-curated narratives concept, so Wave 3 should preserve only the clean current-vocabulary tensions.
